title: Conflict-Based Search with Symmetry Reasoning for Multi-Agent Path Finding
abstract: Multi-Agent Path Finding (MAPF) asks for collision-free paths for a team of agents on a shared graph. Conflict-Based Search (CBS) is a leading optimal solver, but it can explode on instances with many symmetric conflicts. We introduce symmetry-breaking constraints that detect rectangle and corridor symmetries and resolve them with a single high-level branching step. Experiments on standard warehouse and game benchmarks show up to an order-of-magnitude reduction in node expansions and runtime while preserving optimality.

title: Lifelong Multi-Agent Pickup and Delivery in Automated Warehouses via Priority Learning
abstract: In automated warehouses, robots continually receive pickup-and-delivery tasks (MAPD). We propose a learning-based priority assignment that combines a windowed planner with a graph neural network predicting congestion. The method scales to hundreds of agents and improves throughput over rule-based priorities, while keeping planning latency low enough for real-time deployment.
